  "active_set": [
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For improving this methodology draft, do you want A) line-level polishing only (grammar, clarity, concision) without altering claims or structure, B) deeper section-level reorganization and conceptual reframing of the methodology and claims, or C) both A and B?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.5": "Section-level reorganization may improve the flow of the methodology, clarify the sequence of pipeline stages, and strengthen transitions between steps and results.",
    "R1.6": "Line-editing is central to enhance clarity, concision, and readability while fixing grammar and stylistic issues.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve and clearly communicate the uncertainty statements (e.g., posterior distributions and 89% agreement) without implying unjustified precision.",
    "RX.2": "Revisions must avoid overstating robustness or performance beyond what the reported benchmarking supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within improving the provided text and not introduce new analyses, data, or claims.",
    "RX.4": "Language should not encourage misuse or misinterpretation of the methodology, minimizing potential downstream miscommunication.",
    "RX.5": "Any edits must maintain traceability to the authors’ reported results and benchmarking figures to preserve provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For improving this methodology draft, do you want A) line-level polishing only (grammar, clarity, concision) without altering claims or structure, B) deeper section-level reorganization and conceptual reframing of the methodology and claims, or C) both A and B?

rationale.R1.5: Section-level reorganization may improve the flow of the methodology, clarify the sequence of pipeline stages, and strengthen transitions between steps and results.
rationale.R1.6: Line-editing is central to enhance clarity, concision, and readability while fixing grammar and stylistic issues.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve and clearly communicate the uncertainty statements (e.g., posterior distributions and 89% agreement) without implying unjustified precision.
rationale.RX.2: Revisions must avoid overstating robustness or performance beyond what the reported benchmarking supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within improving the provided text and not introduce new analyses, data, or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Language should not encourage misuse or misinterpretation of the methodology, minimizing potential downstream miscommunication.
rationale.RX.5: Any edits must maintain traceability to the authors’ reported results and benchmarking figures to preserve provenance.
